{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/123814?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/taviso/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/taviso/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/taviso/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/taviso/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/taviso", "id": 123814, "login": "taviso", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjEyMzgxNA==", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/taviso/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/taviso/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/taviso/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/taviso/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/taviso/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/taviso", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-59153", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 8.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:H/VI:N/VA:N/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-22", "name": "Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-346", "name": "Origin Validation Error"}], "description": "## Summary

Anki launches a local HTTP server to serve media files and web pages for parts of its interface. While the server has a CORS setup, requests from other origins were not blocked, allowing malicious websites to potentially trigger side-effecting requests.

## Browser impact

The severity varies by browser because of Private Network Access (PNA), a newer spec that restricts web pages from making requests to localhost/local network addresses:

Chrome/Chromium (including Edge, Brave): Largely protected, as Chrome has implemented PNA restrictions for several years and now puts local network access behind a permission prompt.
Safari: Hasn't implemented PNA yet, though macOS has some OS-level protections.
Firefox: Most vulnerable — hasn't implemented PNA yet, though it's reportedly planned for Firefox 151.

## Patches

The issue was fixed as of Anki 25.09.3

### References

title: Anki's local HTTP server does not sufficiently validate requests
